danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures positioned in level paths
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring evasion or combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Obstacles, pitfalls, and stage-based traps in linear traversal areas
Distinct stage culmination areas with boss entity presence
Game over state triggered after checkpoint respawn exhaustion
danger_signals.meaning: Campaign failure; restart or continue required

safety_signals.visual_form: Reached during stage progression; enables respawn without full restart
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progress point; player resources preserved if defeated
Health restoration and resource acquisition for combat readiness

safety_signals.visual_form: Pickups for sling stones, boomerangs, explosives
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced combat capability; safe to engage threats
Campaign continuity; player agency over retry strategy

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player character sprite in Mayan jungle environments

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Evil Mayan warrior spirit identity; implied dark/supernatural visual treatment
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss encounter entity with distinct visual presentation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Captured Harry Sr. as captive; central narrative location at Uaxactun treasure site
Jungle of Central America setting; Mayan empire architecture and aesthetics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large, linear stage layouts with clear forward traversal paths
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player navigates straightforward obstacle and enemy sequences; minimal exploration required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Maze-like, exploratory stage designs with multiple route options
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must evaluate different paths to reach objectives; increased agency in navigation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct arena zone with boss entity present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High-difficulty combat required; checkpoint system ensures safe retry access

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pitfalls, obstacles, environmental traps integrated into stage design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Precision platforming required; damage or death on contact

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Narrative framing of Uaxactun location; Zakelua presence as captor
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player motivation to progress; rescue objective drives campaign narrative

environmental_storytelling.example: Uaxactun treasure site as central point where father is captured; communicates world danger and quest stakes
Harry Jr. as son of legendary adventurer Harry Sr.; inherited whip weapon and exploration drive signals generational continuity
Mayan jungle of Central America with period-appropriate architecture and enemy types; establishes world credibility and cultural context
Stage progression from linear combat to maze-like exploration mirrors narrative escalation toward final confrontation with Zakelua
Letters spelling 'Pitfall' and hidden collectibles encourage environmental observation; rewards thorough world engagement

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Whip attacks, projectile weaponry (sling stones, boomerangs, explosives); charging mechanics for power shots
Running, jumping, climbing, swinging across stage elements; crouching for enclosed area navigation
Respawn at nearest checkpoint with preserved lives and collected items
Positioned in linear stage paths; require evasion or combat elimination for progression
Distinct arena presence; requires sustained combat until defeat; guards progression to next stage
Pitfalls and obstacles remain constant; instant damage/death on player contact

notes: Pitfall: The Mayan Adventure uses hand-drawn animation and sprite-based visuals (per Kroyer Films collaboration) to communicate world state primarily through environmental design progression (linear→maze), enemy/hazard placement, and checkpoint/item systems. Faction communication relies on character visual identity (whip-wielding adventurer vs. supernatural Mayan warrior spirit antagonist) rather than explicit color-coding or sigils. World state manifests through stage layout architecture and narrative framing around the Uaxactun treasure and father's captivity.
